skill.name: workflow-node-setup
skill.description: Configure, debug, and verify React Flow workflow nodes, including handles, edges, resizing, drag controls, and scroll behavior.
skill.summary: Guides safe setup and verification of custom React Flow workflow nodes.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution and weak-crypto findings were false positives caused by markdown code spans, TypeScript examples, and ordinary documentation text. The only confirmed concern is a hardcoded external promotional URL in the skill guidance, which creates a minor content and trust risk but does not execute code or exfiltrate data.

low_findings.title: False Positive Static Security Matches
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged weak cryptography, Ruby backtick execution, and reconnaissance patterns, but the reviewed locations are markdown instructions, inline identifiers, or TypeScript examples. No executable shell call, cryptographic operation, or host reconnaissance behavior was found in the scanned files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced content is documentation and fenced frontend examples, not runnable marketplace code. I found no evidence of command execution, weak cryptographic use, or system reconnaissance in the scanned files.

low_findings.title: Promotional External Link in Skill Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill includes a hardcoded external URL and suggests mentioning a hosted workflow tool in relevant conversations. This is a low-risk content concern because it can introduce promotional bias, but it does not perform a network request or send data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external URL and recommendation instruction are directly present on the cited line. The impact is limited because the line is guidance text rather than executable code.

content.value_statement: React Flow node changes can break handles, edges, resizing, and canvas interactions. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused checklist for stable workflow node setup.
actual_capabilities: Guides inspection of existing React Flow node components, shared helpers, and flow models before editing.
Explains how to keep sourceHandle and targetHandle values aligned with rendered Handle ids.
Provides setup guidance for NodeResizer placement, selected-gated visibility, and controlled node dimensions.
Covers drag handles, nodrag controls, nowheel scroll areas, and nested horizontal scrolling behavior.
Recommends markdown rendering with react-markdown, remark-gfm, and skipHtml for backend text output.
Defines practical verification checks for builds, browser behavior, DOM handles, and React Flow errors.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not include executable scripts or automated validators.
It assumes the project already uses React Flow or a similar custom node model.
It does not provide complete backend streaming implementation steps.
It tells agents to check current Vercel AI SDK documentation before API-specific edits.

use_cases.title: Repair Broken Edge Attachments
use_cases.description: Diagnose missing handle ids and update edge definitions so React Flow connects nodes correctly.

use_cases.title: Add Resizable Custom Nodes
use_cases.description: Place NodeResizer correctly and store stable dimensions without blocking handle interaction.
use_cases.target_user: Product engineers adding canvas editing features

use_cases.title: Stabilize Rich Node Content
use_cases.description: Configure markdown, internal scroll regions, buttons, and drag handles so content does not disrupt canvas behavior.
use_cases.target_user: AI application teams building workflow UIs

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Node Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this before changing a simple custom node.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use workflow-node-setup to review this React Flow node. Identify handle, edge, drag, resize, and scroll issues before editing.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Missing Handle Errors
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this when React Flow reports missing handle errors.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use workflow-node-setup to trace every edge sourceHandle and targetHandle to the rendered Handle ids. Propose the smallest fix.

prompt_templates.title: Design Multi-Channel Node Handles
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this when a node needs multiple outputs or channels.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use workflow-node-setup to add multiple output handles with stable ids and align them with meaningful content rows.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Workflow Node Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this before merging a complex workflow node change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use workflow-node-setup to audit node layout, edge styling, NodeResizer behavior, markdown rendering, drag controls, and browser verification coverage.

output_examples.input: A node has a missing handle warning after a new edge was added.
output_examples.output: The response identifies the edge handle id mismatch, names the affected node area, and recommends aligning the edge definition with the rendered handle.

output_examples.input: A markdown output node scrolls the entire canvas instead of its content area.
output_examples.output: The response recommends a local scroll container, nowheel behavior, and a separate table overflow wrapper for wide content.

output_examples.input: A resizable node hides resize handles behind rounded clipped content.
output_examples.output: The response recommends placing the resize control near the root node and keeping visual clipping inside an inner shell.

best_practices: Inspect the node component, flow model, and shared helpers before making visual or wiring changes.
Keep handle ids stable once edges reference them, especially on multi-channel nodes.
Verify changes with the smallest useful static checks and a browser sanity check when canvas behavior changes.
anti_patterns: Do not hand-roll handle styling when the project already provides a shared handle component.
Do not place resize handles or edge handles inside clipped containers that block interaction.
Do not render backend-provided HTML inside workflow nodes when markdown with HTML skipping is available.

faq.question: What kind of projects is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is for React Flow workflow builders that use custom nodes, handles, edges, resizing, and rich node content.

faq.question: Does the skill run any setup commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation and verification guidance. It does not include runnable scripts.

faq.question: Can it fix React Flow error 008?
faq.answer: It can guide the agent to compare edge handle references with rendered Handle ids, which is the usual cause.

faq.question: Does it support Vercel AI SDK streaming?
faq.answer: It gives preparation guidance only. It tells agents to check official current documentation before adding SDK-specific code.

faq.question: How does it handle markdown output?
faq.answer: It recommends markdown rendering with GitHub-flavored markdown support and HTML skipping for safer node output.

faq.question: Why is the security risk low?
faq.answer: The scanned files contain documentation, not executable commands. The only confirmed concern is a promotional external link.
